name: Mundell Lowe
abstract: James Mundell Lowe (April 21, 1922 – December 2, 2017) was an American jazz guitarist who worked often in radio, television, and film, and as a session musician. He produced film and TV scores in the 1970s, such as the Billy Jack soundtrack and music for Starsky and Hutch, and worked with André Previn's Trio in the 1990s.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: I was a member of the original Sauter-Finegan Orchestra... Mundell Lowe played guitar
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: The Bobby Tucker Quintet consisted of Denzil along with Mundell Lowe on guitar (Remo couldn't make it), Tony Scott on clarinet, Bobby and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: The first six records with Dion and the Belmonts I had Mundell Lowe was on guitar
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: He was there, and Mundell Lowe, and somebody else. We were playing together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first artists that Riverside got involved with was Mundell Lowe... Mundell wanted me and Bill Grauer both to get on the phone at the same time, and he proceeded to play a tape of a piano player he wanted us to be interested in.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Lateano
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Lyons... and the then- musical director Mundell Lowe got the idea to put music education in Monterey County... So they started with three clinicians: myself, Bill Berry
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Lateano interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002
